howItWorks.intro: This strategic petroleum reserve depletion timeline uses established formulas to provide accurate results.
rules: Days of coverage = SPR level / effective daily release rate
Effective release = min(max drawdown rate, daily supply gap)
Daily supply gap = US daily use × disruption %
howItWorks.outro: Results are estimates. Consult a professional for critical decisions.

faq.question: How much oil is in the Strategic Petroleum Reserve?
faq.answer: As of early 2024, the SPR holds approximately 370 million barrels, down from a peak of 727 million in 2009. The Biden administration released 180 million barrels in 2022 and has been slowly refilling.

faq.question: What is the maximum SPR release rate?
faq.answer: The maximum drawdown rate is 4.4 million barrels per day, though actual releases have typically been 1-2 million bpd to avoid flooding the market.

faq.question: When was the SPR created?
faq.answer: The SPR was established in 1975 after the 1973-74 Arab oil embargo. It's stored in salt caverns along the Gulf Coast in Texas and Louisiana.

faq.question: Is the Strategic Petroleum Reserve Depletion Timeline free to use?
faq.answer: Yes, completely free with no signup required. Use it as many times as you need — there are no limits or hidden fees.

faq.question: How accurate is this calculator?
faq.answer: This calculator uses standard geopolitics and energy markets formulas trusted by analysts and informed citizens. Results are reliable estimates for planning purposes. For critical decisions, we recommend consulting a qualified professional to verify.

sections.title: What Is the Strategic Petroleum Reserve Depletion Timeline?
sections.body: The Strategic Petroleum Reserve Depletion Timeline is a free online tool designed for analysts and informed citizens who need quick, accurate calculations in the geopolitics and energy markets space. By entering your current spr level, us daily oil use, import disruption, you get instant results including days of coverage, months of coverage, daily supply gap. No formulas to memorize, no spreadsheets to build — just enter your numbers and get the answer in seconds. Whether you're a beginner or experienced professional, this calculator saves you time and eliminates guesswork.

sections.title: Why This Calculation Matters
sections.body: Getting days of coverage right can make the difference between success and costly mistakes. In geopolitics and energy markets, small errors compound quickly. Manual calculations are error-prone and time-consuming, especially under pressure. This calculator applies proven formulas used by analysts and informed citizens worldwide, giving you confidence that your numbers are correct. Use it to understand global dynamics with precision and avoid common pitfalls that trip up beginners.

sections.title: When Should You Use This Calculator?
sections.body: This tool is most useful when you know your current spr level and need to find the right days of coverage. It's also great for quick estimates before committing to a decision, and to double-check manual calculations or professional quotes, and when comparing different scenarios side by side. Bookmark this page and come back whenever you need a fast, reliable answer — the calculator is always free and requires no signup.

howToSteps.title: Enter Your Current SPR Level (million barrels)
howToSteps.description: Start by entering your current spr level — this is the primary input for the calculation.

howToSteps.title: Fill In Additional Details
howToSteps.description: Complete the remaining fields: us daily oil use, import disruption, max spr release rate. Each value refines the calculation for greater accuracy.

howToSteps.title: Click Calculate
howToSteps.description: Hit the Calculate button to run the numbers. Results appear instantly below.

howToSteps.title: Review Your Results
howToSteps.description: Check your days of coverage, months of coverage, daily supply gap. Use these figures to inform your next decision or compare against alternative scenarios.

tips: Double-check your current spr level before calculating — even small input errors can significantly change your results.
Run the calculator with different values to compare scenarios and find the optimal approach for your situation.
Pay attention to both days of coverage and months of coverage — they work together to give you the full picture.
Bookmark this page for quick access next time you need to understand global dynamics.
If you're unsure about your max spr release rate, start with a conservative estimate and adjust from there.
